mission.title: Install and Configure DigitalOcean Kubernetes (DOKS)
mission.description: DigitalOcean Kubernetes (DOKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters. It is ideal for users looking for a cost-effective and easy-to-use solution for container orchestration without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include limited customization compared to self-managed solutions and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the DigitalOcean CLI (doctl)
steps.description:
This step installs the DigitalOcean command line interface, which is necessary for managing your DOKS clusters.

```bash

brew install doctl

snap install doctl

```

> For macOS users. Use the appropriate package manager for your OS.
> For Ubuntu users.

steps.title: Authenticate doctl
steps.description:
This step authenticates the DigitalOcean CLI with your DigitalOcean account using an API token.

```bash

doctl auth init --access-token YOUR_DIGITALOCEAN_API_TOKEN

```

> Replace YOUR_DIGITALOCEAN_API_TOKEN with your actual API token.

steps.title: Create a DOKS Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new DOKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster create my-cluster --version v1.30.0 --count 3 --region nyc1

```

> Change 'my-cluster' to your desired cluster name.
commands: doctl k8s cluster create my-cluster --version v1.30.0 --count 3 --region nyc1

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created DOKS cluster.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster

```

> Ensure 'my-cluster' matches the name of your created cluster.
commands: doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in your DOKS cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations like setting up autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/digitalocean/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

```

> This sets up the cluster autoscaler for DigitalOcean.

uninstall.title: Delete the DOKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the DOKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster delete my-cluster --force

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

uninstall.title: Remove kubeconfig
uninstall.description:
This step removes the kubeconfig entry for the deleted cluster.

```bash

kubectl config delete-context my-cluster

```

> Ensure 'my-cluster' matches the name of your deleted cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the DOKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades the DOKS cluster to the latest version.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster upgrade my-cluster --version v1.30.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
> Verify that all nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' status.
commands: doctl k8s cluster upgrade my-cluster --version v1.30.0

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unauthorized
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API token used for authentication is invalid or expired.

**Fix:**
Generate a new API token from the DigitalOcean dashboard and re-authenticate using 'doctl auth init --access-token NEW_TOKEN'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: No resources found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified cluster does not exist or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster name and ensure it exists using 'doctl k8s cluster list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: context not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig context for the cluster is missing.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster' to regenerate the kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Insufficient quota
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your DigitalOcean account has reached its resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check your account's resource limits and either delete unused resources or upgrade your account.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 features including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure all workloads are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, confirming that your DOKS cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: doctl k8s cluster create my-cluster --version v1.30.0 --count 3 --region nyc1
doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
